mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with rate declines (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack intensifies, suppressing wage growth expectations and consumer spending forecasts, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings revisions turn downward.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, invalidating the negative earnings revision channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer and business demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns with an out-of-sample accuracy exceeding 55 percent, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side bottlenecks and inventory drawdowns, causing short-term equity sell-offs as markets price in near-term output constraints and margin compression.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of instances following a monthly INDPRO decline, or the signal predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 5 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index surpasses 270, inflation expectations become entrenched, forcing markets to anticipate aggressive central bank tightening that raises discount rates and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60 percent of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 270, or the signal shows zero incremental predictive value over a constant baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to yield a positive rank IC over any 24-month rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with the joint labor tightening condition.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect immediate construction sector activity and downstream consumer spending commitments, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns as markets price in near-term economic momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of months following a positive HOUST revision, or the series exhibits a rank IC below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production during periods of stable monetary policy indicates healthy capacity utilization and corporate output expansion, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns as earnings forecasts are upgraded.
failure_condition: The positive return signal reverses to negative when FEDFUNDS changes by more than 0.25 percent in a single month, or the hypothesis fails to achieve a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 in a long-short portfolio implementation.
